what_it_does: Defines window.CV_HOST, a browser-side registry of pluggable 'runtimes' (ways to reach the world: a no-disk 'review' sandbox floor, the browser File System Access API 'fsapi', and an injected 'native'/MCP host) plus a uniform read/list/write op surface that resolves the best available runtime per capability. Also holds a serializer registry that turns registry mutations into exact source text, a localStorage-persisted proposed-change store (propose/apply/reject), a commit() call that stages-then-optionally-writes a change, handoff modes (review/stash/download), and a describe() self-documentation projection consumed by the UI.
observations: Three runtimes registered with ranks 0/1/2 and explicit caps {read,write,list,exec,tools}; best(cap) picks the highest-rank available runtime that has the cap.
'review' runtime is always available() and has no caps — the explicit degraded floor so the system is never dead.

fsapi runtime uses window.showDirectoryPicker, persists the granted FileSystemDirectoryHandle in IndexedDB ('cv-host' db, 'kv' store), and attempts to restore it on load, surfacing window.__cvFsapiPending for a 'reconnect' gesture.
native runtime reads window.CV_HOST_NATIVE; reqNative throws a specific message naming the missing op and how to implement it.
writerOrThrow() composes an error that lists every write-capable runtime and its activation description.
resolveProviderRuntime bridges to CV_AI: handles 'host-fs' (repo.* capabilities) and 'native-model'/'mcp-model' kinds, else returns null to let CV_AI raise its own unknown-kind error.
commit() always proposes (stages) first; only writes immediately when canWrite() AND autoApply() are both true; otherwise stashes or downloads per handoffMode.
applySerialized supports three strategies: 'new-file', 'css-token' (insert in a selector), and default 'append-block' (insert before an anchor sentinel or at EOF).
fnReplacer serializes function values as 'ƒ'+source string when cloning payloads.
console.info logs the resolved mode label when the IIFE finishes.
standouts: capability-available = f(environment, runtime): a single resolution rule replaces bespoke per-surface file I/O.
Loud-never-silent discipline — every impossible op throws a message naming what is missing and how to activate it; the only graceful path is the explicitly-labelled 'review' staging.
describe() is the single source for the environment's status; the comment forbids any hand-written status string elsewhere.
conventions: IIFE attaching a single global namespace object (window.CV_HOST), 'use strict', no module system.
Mirrors the stated four-registry shape (register/resolve/subscribe/describe) called out in the project CLAUDE.md.
Persistence keys namespaced 'cv:host:*'; localStorage reads wrapped in try/catch returning safe defaults.
concerns: localStorage writes in saveChanges/setHandoffMode swallow errors silently (empty catch) while reads default quietly — a quota failure would lose a staged change without a loud signal, which sits in tension with the file's own loud-never-silent law.
safeClone falls back to returning the original value on JSON failure, so a non-cloneable payload could be stored by reference.
notes: The environment/host capability registry — resolves the best runtime to actually read/write disk from the browser, stages every change review-first, and self-describes the whole environment via describe().
questions: What is the exact contract window.CV_HOST_NATIVE must implement (read/list/write/exec/tool/complete) — is it specified elsewhere?
Who registers serializers and where are the change 'kinds' defined (host-serializer.js is referenced but not in this file)?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the header describes an environment/host registry with pluggable runtimes, uniform routing, loud failure, and a self-documenting describe(), and the code implements exactly that.
apparent_themes: Registry-as-truth applied to the runtime environment
Explicit graceful degradation (labelled sandbox mode, never silent fallback)
Self-describing system (UI as projection of describe())
intention_signals: Trying to make 'what can Vi do to the world it runs in' a single resolvable capability question, so adding a new way to reach disk (Tauri, MCP, cloud) is a runtime registration rather than edits to every caller, and so the running app honestly shows its own degraded vs. full state.
novelty: Treating filesystem/exec/tool access as a ranked capability registry with a guaranteed always-available no-op floor, plus an IndexedDB-persisted File System Access handle with reconnect, and serialize-then-stage commit semantics in a pure browser context.
contribution: Contributes a browser-resident host-capability abstraction: ranked pluggable runtimes, a uniform read/list/write surface, review-first change staging with serializers, IndexedDB-persisted FS Access handles, and a single describe() environment projection — the bridge between an in-browser app and real disk/exec/tool access.
summary_for_embedding: A browser CV_HOST registry of ranked pluggable runtimes (review sandbox, File System Access API, native/MCP host) exposing a uniform read/list/write op surface, a serializer-backed review-first change store, handoff modes, and a self-describing describe() projection.
intention_for_embedding: To make environment capabilities (file read/write, exec, tool calls) a single resolvable question so new ways to reach the world are registered not hand-wired, with loud failure and an honest, self-documented degraded mode.
